message:
chore: close beads ft-3681t.9.3 and ft-3681t.8.2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id": "ft-3681t.7.6", "title": "[ops] Disaster recovery backup/restore and control-plane continuity drills", "description": "Build and validate backup/restore and continuity procedures for mux/orchestration/policy/connector state so catastrophic failures have a proven recovery path.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. Backup and restore flows cover all critical control-plane data stores and metadata. 2. Recovery-time and data-loss objectives are measured and met in drills. 3. Continuity procedures are automatable and operator-verified. Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic. Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario. Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case. Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code. Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:34:51.794904Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-11T22:33:57Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["backup", "disaster-recovery", "ops", "reliability"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "ft-3681t.7.6", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.2.5", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:35:23.746080Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.7.6", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.5.9", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:35:24.326369Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.7.6", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.6.4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:35:24.917771Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.7.6", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.7", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:34:51.794904Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.7.6", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.7.3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:35:25.651055Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "assignee": "PinkForge"}
 {"id": "ft-3681t.8", "title": "[epic] Migration, cutover, and adoption program", "description": "# Epic 8: Migration, Cutover, and Adoption

Drive a disciplined transition from NTM-centered operations to FrankenTerm-native operations with parity corpus, dual-run comparison, data migration, staged cutover, and decommission path.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. Migration path is low-risk and reversible until final cutover.
2. Parity and shadow validation prove behavioral confidence.
3. Operator/contributor docs support full adoption and ntm retirement.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:09:47.164818Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-02T19:31:55.939064Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["adoption", "cutover", "epic", "migration"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:09:47.164818Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:09:52.570936Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:09:52.936223Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.5", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:09:53.327500Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.6", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:09:53.709227Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.7", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:09:54.084765Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.9", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:23:57.517549Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1725, "issue_id": "ft-3681t.8", "author": "Jeff Emanuel", "text": "Design note: Migration is staged and evidence-driven. Shadow comparison and parity corpus are required so cutover is a controlled transition, not a leap of faith. Decommission of NTM dependency is explicit and auditable.", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:17:58Z"}]}
 {"id": "ft-3681t.8.1", "title": "[migration] NTM parity corpus and acceptance matrix", "description": "Create a corpus of canonical NTM workflows and expected outcomes to serve as objective parity and regression checks for FrankenTerm-native replacement.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. Corpus spans critical NTM operational scenarios and edge cases. 2. Expected outcomes are machine-verifiable. 3. Corpus is reusable for CI/regression.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "GrayRiver", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:14:40.775331Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-04T03:15:48.989687Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-04T03:15:48.989634Z", "close_reason": "Delivered v1 NTM parity corpus + machine-verifiable acceptance matrix artifacts and execution-plan linkage.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["migration", "parity", "validation"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8.1", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.3.5", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:17:29.401540Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8.1", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.4.4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:17:28.923658Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8.1", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.5.9", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:17:29.889086Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8.1", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.8", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:14:40.775331Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8.1", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.9.2", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:28:42.943969Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1852, "issue_id": "ft-3681t.8.1", "author": "GrayRiver", "text": "Draft parity artifacts added: docs/ntm-parity-corpus-ft-3681t.8.1.md, fixtures/e2e/ntm_parity/corpus.v1.json, fixtures/e2e/ntm_parity/acceptance_matrix.v1.json; plus execution plan linkage in docs/ft-3681t-execution-plan.md. Corpus includes 12 canonical NTM-equivalent scenarios with machine-verifiable assertion contracts and gate matrix for downstream 8.2/8.4 consumption. JSON artifacts validated with jq.", "created_at": "2026-03-04T03:15:10Z"}]}
-{"id": "ft-3681t.8.2", "title": "[migration] Dual-run shadow comparator (NTM vs FrankenTerm)", "description": "Implement shadow-mode evaluation that compares outcomes/latency/failure signatures between NTM and FrankenTerm for identical scenarios before irreversible cutover.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. Comparator highlights semantic and performance deltas. 2. Drift triage workflow is defined. 3. Shadow results feed cutover gates.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.

Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.", "status": "in_progress", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "PinkForge", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:14:45.910771Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-11T23:00:00.000000Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["migration", "shadow-mode", "validation"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8.2", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.8", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:14:45.910771Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8.2", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.8.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:17:30.394323Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1943, "issue_id": "ft-3681t.8.2", "author": "BlueHorse", "text": "Claiming ft-3681t.8.2. Planned slice: wire the existing NTM parity corpus + acceptance matrix into a runnable shadow comparator that executes canonical ft commands, evaluates assertion contracts, and emits the artifact bundle expected by the cutover playbook (summary.json, raw_command_outputs.jsonl, assertion_results.json, divergence report).", "created_at": "2026-03-07T07:57:07Z"}, {"id": 1951, "issue_id": "ft-3681t.8.2", "author": "BlueHorse", "text": "Progress update: implemented a first `ft replay parity` runner in `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` that loads the canonical NTM parity corpus + acceptance matrix, auto-resolves a live pane for `<pane_id>` substitutions, executes the canonical `ft` commands via the current binary, evaluates assertion contracts via `frankenterm_core::ntm_parity`, and writes `summary.json`, `raw_command_outputs.jsonl`, `assertion_results.json`, `shadow_divergence_report.json`, plus per-scenario artifacts under `scenarios/`.

Added a fixture-driven integration test in `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/ntm_parity_runner.rs` and a CLI parse test for `ft replay parity`.

Cargo validation is currently blocked by infrastructure: `rch workers probe --all` shows every worker unreachable, and `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` fails open to local compilation, which I aborted to stay within repo policy.
", "created_at": "2026-03-07T08:16:12Z"}]}

 {"id": "ft-3681t.8.4", "title": "[migration] Staged cutover playbook with rollback gates", "description": "Define and automate a staged rollout strategy by team/fleet/environment with progressive canary exposure, blast-radius controls, and rollback checkpoints tied to reliability/security/perf gates.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. Cutover phases and promotion criteria are explicit per environment/team cohort.
2. Progressive exposure (canary cohorts + blast-radius limits) is enforced with automatic halt on guardrail breaches.
3. Rollback triggers are objective, continuously evaluated, and executable within bounded recovery time.
4. Playbook is validated in rehearsals including partial-failure modes and human override drills with signed run logs.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5.  {"id": "ft-3681t.8.5", "title": "[migration] NTM decommission + full operator/contributor documentation", "description": "Finalize adoption: retire NTM operational dependency, publish operator playbooks, and document contributor workflows for native mux and connector development.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. Operational dependency on NTM is removed. 2. Documentation supports day-2 operation and contributor onboarding. 3. Decommission is auditable and reversible only by explicit policy.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:15:03.305039Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-02T20:01:23.331178Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["decommission", "documentation", "migration"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8.5", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.6.6", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:35:31.903414Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8.5", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.7.6", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:35:32.864454Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8.5", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.8", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:15:03.305039Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8.5", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.8.4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:17:34.455365Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8.5", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.8.6", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:28:50.634678Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.8.5", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.9.6", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T20:01:23.331112Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
